div.p: Step-by-step mount table transformation for container filesystem isolation

g.text: Mount ID | Source | Mount Point | Type
newroot is now a mount point ✓
Mount ID | Source | Mount Point | Type
Roots swapped atomically under namespace_sem lock
Step 3: Change to New Root Directory
Mount ID | Source | Mount Point | Type
Step 4: Unmount Old Root with MNT_DETACH
Mount ID | Source | Mount Point | Type
mount(newroot, newroot, NULL, MS_BIND | MS_REC, NULL);

text.tspan: // Creates mount entry without changing content
// Required: pivot_root needs newroot to be a mount point
// Creates mount entry without changing content
// Required: pivot_root needs newroot to be a mount point
// Create oldroot directory inside newroot first
// Atomic swap: old root → oldroot_dir, newroot → /
// Create oldroot directory inside newroot first
// Atomic swap: old root → oldroot_dir, newroot → /
// Now process cwd is in the new root
// Now process cwd is in the new root
// MNT_DETACH: lazy unmount, waits for refs to drop
// After this: host filesystem is GONE from container view
// MNT_DETACH: lazy unmount, waits for refs to drop
// After this: host filesystem is GONE from container view

g.text: • newroot MUST be a mount point (bind-mount-to-self trick)
• Caller needs CAP_SYS_ADMIN in user namespace
• Mount propagation should be set to MS_PRIVATE first
• All operations atomic under kernel's namespace_sem lock
